name: Andhra Pradesh
intro: A coastal gem in southern India known for the sacred hill of Tirumala, serene beaches of Visakhapatnam, and rich Buddhist heritage at Amaravati.
travelNotes: Summers can be extremely hot. Coastal areas are humid year-round.

name: Arunachal Pradesh
intro: India's north-eastern frontier with mist-laden valleys, vibrant tribal cultures, and the breathtaking Tawang Monastery perched at 10,000 feet.
travelNotes: Inner Line Permit (ILP) required for all Indian tourists. Roads may be disrupted during monsoon.

name: Assam
intro: Famed for its lush tea gardens, the mighty Brahmaputra, one-horned rhinos at Kaziranga, and the world's largest river island, Majuli.
travelNotes: Carry rain gear even in shoulder season. Kaziranga is closed during monsoon (May-Sep).

name: Bihar
intro: The cradle of Buddhism and Jainism, home to the ancient ruins of Nalanda, the Bodhi Tree at Bodh Gaya, and a rich tapestry of folk traditions.
travelNotes: Summers are very hot; winters can be foggy. Stick to well-known tourist areas.

name: Chhattisgarh
intro: An unspoilt state with Chitrakote Falls — India's widest waterfall, ancient cave paintings at Kabra Pahad, and dense Sal forests.
travelNotes: Some interior regions have restricted access. Check advisories before travelling to Bastar.

name: Goa
intro: India's smallest state, and its most popular beach destination. Portuguese cathedrals, vibrant nightlife, spice plantations, and golden sunsets over the Arabian Sea.
travelNotes: Monsoon (Jun–Sep) brings heavy rains and many beach shacks close.

name: Gujarat
intro: From the white desert of Kutch to the sacred temples of Somnath and Dwarka, Gujarat blends industry, heritage, and wildlife (Gir National Park is home to Asiatic lions).
travelNotes: Gujarat is a dry state — alcohol is prohibited without a permit.

name: Haryana
intro: A gateway to northern India with the Kurukshetra battlefield, Sultanpur bird sanctuary, and rapidly growing cities alongside ancient heritage sites.
travelNotes: Well connected by road from Delhi. Summers are hot and dusty.

name: Himachal Pradesh
intro: Snow-capped peaks, apple orchards, ancient temples, and charming hill stations. From the bustling Mall Road in Shimla to the serene monasteries of Spiti, every valley tells a story.
travelNotes: Roads are prone to landslides during monsoon (Jul–Aug). Carry warm clothing even in summer for high-altitude areas.

name: Jharkhand
intro: Rich tribal heritage, dense forests, and stunning waterfalls like Hundru and Dassam. Parasnath Hill is an important Jain pilgrimage site.
travelNotes: Infrastructure is basic outside Ranchi. Plan transport in advance.

name: Karnataka
intro: Tech-hub Bengaluru meets the ruins of Hampi, the coffee estates of Coorg, the beaches of Gokarna, and Mysuru's royal grandeur — all in one state.
travelNotes: Coastal Karnataka gets heavy monsoon rain. Hill stations like Coorg are best avoided in peak monsoon.

name: Kerala
intro: Emerald backwaters, Ayurvedic retreats, tea-blanketed hills of Munnar, and the pristine beaches of Varkala. Kerala perfects the art of slow, soulful travel.
travelNotes: Monsoon season (Jun–Aug) brings Ayurveda tourism but heavy downpours. Houseboat availability peaks Oct–Jan.

name: Madhya Pradesh
intro: Tiger trails in Bandhavgarh, the erotic sculptures of Khajuraho, the rock shelters of Bhimbetka, and the holy city of Ujjain — truly the heart of India.
travelNotes: National parks close during monsoon (Jul–Sep). Book jungle safaris in advance.

name: Maharashtra
intro: From Mumbai's iconic skyline and the rock-cut caves of Ajanta & Ellora to the Sahyadri forts and serene Konkan coast — Maharashtra is endlessly diverse.
travelNotes: Mumbai is expensive compared to other Indian cities. Monsoon treks in Sahyadris are thrilling but risky.

name: Manipur
intro: Home to the unique floating market of Loktak Lake, Manipur showcases rich martial-art traditions, vibrant festivals, and verdant valleys.
travelNotes: ILP may be required for some areas. Check current advisories before travel.

name: Meghalaya
intro: Living root bridges, Cherrapunji's record rainfall, crystal-clear rivers, and the unique Khasi culture. A paradise for trekkers and nature lovers.
travelNotes: Heaviest rainfall in India during monsoon. Waterproof gear is essential year-round.

name: Mizoram
intro: Rolling blue hills, Phawngpui (Blue Mountain), bamboo forests, and a warm Mizo hospitality that makes every visitor feel at home.
travelNotes: Inner Line Permit required. Limited tourist infrastructure — plan ahead.

name: Nagaland
intro: Hornbill Festival, warrior tribal traditions, dramatic terraced hills, and the world's hottest chili (Bhut Jolokia). Nagaland is raw, real, and unforgettable.
travelNotes: ILP required. Visit during the Hornbill Festival (December) for the best cultural experience.

name: Odisha
intro: The Sun Temple of Konark, Jagannath Temple in Puri, Chilika Lake's migratory birds, and pristine tribal villages. Odisha is deeply spiritual and artistically rich.
travelNotes: Cyclone season (Oct–Dec) can disrupt coastal travel. Rath Yatra (Jun/Jul) draws massive crowds.

name: Punjab
intro: The Golden Temple of Amritsar, the Wagah Border ceremony, rich Sikh heritage, and some of the most flavourful food in India.
travelNotes: Summers are scorching (up to 45°C). The Golden Temple provides free meals to all visitors.

name: Rajasthan
intro: Majestic forts, vibrant desert festivals, ornate havelis, and a riot of colour at every turn. From Jaipur's pink walls to Jaisalmer's golden sands, Rajasthan is India at its most regal.
travelNotes: Desert regions can exceed 45°C in summer. Always carry water and sun protection.

name: Sikkim
intro: Nestled in the Himalayas with views of Kanchenjunga, Sikkim offers pristine monasteries, flower valleys, and Tsomgo Lake's ethereal beauty.
travelNotes: Restricted Area Permit needed for Nathula Pass and some northern areas. Roads can be slippery in monsoon.

name: Tamil Nadu
intro: Dravidian temple towers, the French Quarter of Pondicherry, Nilgiri hill stations, and the ancient shore temples of Mahabalipuram — Tamil Nadu is heritage at every step.
travelNotes: Cyclone season affects the coast (Oct–Dec). Temples often have dress codes.

name: Telangana
intro: Hyderabad's Charminar, world-class biryanis, the Golconda Fort, and the serene Nagarjuna Sagar dam — Telangana blends Nizam-era grandeur with modern energy.
travelNotes: Summers are hot and humid. Old City areas can be crowded — plan visits early morning.

name: Tripura
intro: Neermahal water palace, Unakoti rock carvings, and the royal palaces of Agartala. A compact, peaceful state often missed on the tourist trail.
travelNotes: Limited flight connectivity. Road travel from Assam is the common entry point.

name: Uttar Pradesh
intro: Home to the Taj Mahal, the ghats of Varanasi, Lucknow's Nawabi cuisine, and Sarnath where Buddha gave his first sermon. India's most populous state is layered with history.
travelNotes: Summer heat can be extreme (45°C+). Varanasi ghats get crowded during festivals.

name: Uttarakhand
intro: Char Dham pilgrimage, Jim Corbett's tigers, Rishikesh's yoga ashrams, and the serene Valley of Flowers. Uttarakhand balances adventure and spirituality.
travelNotes: Char Dham route closes in winter (Nov–Apr). Flash floods are a risk during monsoon.

name: West Bengal
intro: Kolkata's colonial charm, Darjeeling's tea gardens with Kanchenjunga views, the Sundarbans mangrove delta, and Shantiniketan's artistic legacy.
travelNotes: Durga Puja (Oct) is the biggest festival — cities are festive but extremely crowded.

name: Andaman & Nicobar Islands
intro: Turquoise waters, pristine coral reefs, colonial history at Cellular Jail, and some of Asia's best beaches. A tropical paradise in the Bay of Bengal.
travelNotes: Restricted Area Permit needed. Nicobar islands are off-limits to tourists.

name: Chandigarh
intro: India's first planned city, designed by Le Corbusier. Rock Garden, Sukhna Lake, and tree-lined sectors make it one of the cleanest cities in India.
travelNotes: Well-connected by road and train from Delhi. A convenient base for exploring Punjab and Himachal.

name: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
intro: A quiet coastal retreat with Portuguese-era churches, Devka Beach, and lush green forests — a lesser-known alternative to Goa.
travelNotes: Alcohol is freely available in Daman & Diu, unlike nearby Gujarat.

name: Delhi
intro: India's capital — seven historic cities layered beneath one metropolis. Red Fort, Qutub Minar, Mughal gardens, and legendary street food from Chandni Chowk.
travelNotes: Air quality is poor in winter (Nov–Jan). Use the Metro to avoid traffic. Summer heat can exceed 45°C.

name: Jammu & Kashmir
intro: Dal Lake houseboats, Mughal gardens, Gulmarg's ski slopes, and the Vaishno Devi pilgrimage — J&K lives up to its title as heaven on earth.
travelNotes: Check security advisories. Winter roads may close due to snowfall. Register with local authorities in remote areas.

name: Ladakh
intro: Dramatic moonscapes, ancient Buddhist monasteries, Pangong Lake's shifting blue hues, and the world's highest motorable passes. Ladakh is a bucket-list essential.
travelNotes: Acclimatise for at least a day at altitude. Road to Leh from Manali/Srinagar opens only in summer.

name: Lakshadweep
intro: India's smallest UT — 36 coral islands with crystal-clear lagoons, untouched beaches, and vibrant marine life perfect for snorkelling and diving.
travelNotes: Entry permit required. Access is by ship from Kochi or limited flights. Book well in advance.

name: Puducherry
intro: Pastel-coloured French Quarter, Auroville, serene promenades, and some of the best cafes in South India. A laid-back coastal gem.
travelNotes: Cyclone season (Nov–Dec) can bring heavy rain. Renting a bicycle is the best way to explore.
